role: It is the client-side tool registry store that fetches and enriches tool descriptors, manages the open/selected/loading state for the tool palette, and exposes a React hook for ToolsBar/ToolsPanel.
capabilities: Fallback to a seeded corpus descriptor when /api/tools is unavailable or empty
Map fork snake_case form_meta into the surface ToolDescriptor shape
Infer the op discriminator field from schema enum values
Resolve dynamic enum options from registry URLs with a 1.5s timeout and graceful degradation
Enrich enum dropdowns in the background without blocking the initial tool list render
Maintain open/selected/loading/error/scaffold state with a subscribe/notify store
Expose useTools React hook plus openTools/closeTools/selectTool actions
Guard against stale fetches using a monotonic load sequence token
substance_why: The file implements real fetch, enrichment, and state management for the tool palette but explicitly defers the run/invoke action and result rendering to pending endpoints and DNA's archetype, and falls back to a single seeded tool when the registry endpoint is absent.
interactions_present: Select a tool from the list (selectTool)
Load the tool list on open/mount (loadTools)
Subscribe React components to store changes (useTools)
interactions_missing: Run/invoke a tool (no /api/tools/invoke wiring or submit handler)
Render tool results (DNA archetype not integrated)
Validate required op params before running
Explicit opField from server metadata (currently inferred)
Form rendering and submission UI (not in this store file)
Error recovery beyond falling back to the seed
performance_risks: Concurrent background enum-source fetches for every tool can spike network usage
No caching of resolved enum sources across store loads
Every state change notifies all subscribers, causing re-renders in every useTools consumer
loadTools can be triggered multiple times, spawning duplicate fetches despite sequence tokens
AbortController timeouts only mitigate slow enum fetches, not overall fetch volume
reuse_why: Keep the form_meta adapter, enum-source resolver, and store contract, but rebuild the global mutable state into a proper React context/reducer or hook and move fetch/enrichment into a dedicated service.
decomposition_hints: Split tool descriptor fetching and caching into a dedicated api/tools service
Move form_meta mapping into a pure adapter module
Move enum-source resolution into a separate registry-options service
Move the seeded corpus fixture into a config/fixtures file
Keep useTools as a thin React binding over the store
Separate the open/selected UI state from tool data state
